name: Cccs Of The Mid-Ohio Valley, Inc.
description_short: CCCS of the Mid-Ohio Valley is a local nonprofit offering free and low-cost financial counseling, debt management, and bankruptcy guidance to residents regardless of income.
description_long:
CCCS of the Mid-Ohio Valley, Inc. is a nonprofit organization serving the Greater Mid-Ohio Valley region with a mission to help individuals and families achieve financial wellness through education and counseling. The organization has established itself as a trusted local resource, having worked with thousands of clients over its operational history. Their approach centers on understanding client circumstances and prioritizing their best interests through personalized guidance.

The organization offers a comprehensive range of services including debt management counseling, bankruptcy counseling, budgeting assistance, financial coaching, HUD housing counseling, and home buyer education. They provide multiple delivery formats including in-person training, online workshops, and individual counseling appointments. Their team holds several certifications relevant to financial counseling and specializes in helping clients with credit card debt, loan repayments, and general debt-related challenges. They emphasize proactive planning for the future, not just crisis intervention.

CCCS of the Mid-Ohio Valley distinguishes itself through income-based guidelines that make services affordable regardless of family size or income level, positioning their services as accessible to the full economic spectrum. Their staff is praised in testimonials for being interactive, responsive, and willing to research answers when uncertain. Client testimonials highlight concrete outcomes, including one case where a debt management program reportedly helped clients become debt-free within three years. The organization also offers financial literacy workshops and maintains an email newsletter for ongoing financial advice.

As a free-help nonprofit, this organization operates within the constraints of nonprofit funding and volunteer/staff availability. The website provides limited detail on specific program timelines, success rates, or the exact nature of their debt management programs. While they clearly serve a legitimate counseling function, potential clients should expect standard nonprofit operations rather than specialized or expedited services. The organization's focus on the Mid-Ohio Valley region may limit accessibility for those outside their service area.
pros: Income-based sliding scale fees make services accessible to all income levels
Staff holds multiple certifications in financial counseling
Offers both debt management and pre-bankruptcy counseling in same organization
Multiple service formats including in-person, online workshops, and individual coaching
HUD housing counseling and home buyer education programs available
Documented case outcomes with clients reporting debt freedom in 3-year programs
Local nonprofit with staff described as responsive and willing to follow up with answers
cons: Limited to Mid-Ohio Valley region; may not serve clients outside this geographic area
Website provides minimal detail on specific program timelines or success rates
No transparency on actual debt management program terms or average outcomes
Generic website content provides limited specifics about counselor qualifications or methodology
Standard nonprofit constraints may result in limited availability or appointment wait times
best_for: Residents of the Mid-Ohio Valley facing credit card debt or general debt management challenges
Individuals considering bankruptcy who need pre-filing counseling and guidance
Low-to-moderate income households seeking affordable financial coaching and budgeting help
First-time homebuyers in the region needing HUD-approved housing counseling
diagnosis: CCCS of the Mid-Ohio Valley is best for Mid-Ohio Valley residents seeking legitimate nonprofit credit counseling and debt management guidance, particularly those with limited income or facing bankruptcy decisions. Primary caveat: services are geographically limited to the Mid-Ohio Valley region, and the website lacks detailed information about specific program outcomes, timelines, or counselor-to-client ratios that would indicate service quality depth.
services: Budgeting assistance and financial literacy training
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
